content: pytest-httpx 0.36.2 is a pytest fixture-based library for mocking httpx requests. It provides a httpx_mock fixture that intercepts all httpx calls made during a test. Key usage: def test_foo(httpx_mock): httpx_mock.add_response(method='POST', url='https://api.github.com/...', json={...}, status_code=201). For async tests with pytest-asyncio, the fixture works identically. pytest-httpx also supports add_exception() for simulating network failures. The fixture approach (vs respx decorator) means mocking is automatically cleaned up after each test. pytest-httpx requires httpx to be installed and is version-coupled to httpx — pytest-httpx 0.36.x is compatible with httpx 0.28.x. Key advantage over respx: simpler API for straightforward request/response mocking, fixture-based cleanup.
